channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Australia shelters 7 Iranian women's soccer team members, but 1 wants to go back
item.description: Australia says it's given asylum to seven members of the Iranian national women's soccer team, but one has asked to return to Iran.
item.content:encoded: Australia says it's given asylum to seven members of the Iranian national women's soccer team, but one has asked to return to Iran.
item.dc:creator: Kristina Kukolja

item.title: Chile turns right: Kast inaugurated as nation's most conservative leader since Pinochet
item.description: Chile has sworn in its most right-wing president in decades — and his rise, and ideology, are rooted in a small town beneath the Andes.
item.content:encoded:
Chile has sworn in its most right-wing president in decades — and his rise, and ideology, are rooted in a small town beneath the Andes.

(Image credit: Gustavo Garello)
item.dc:creator: John Bartlett

item.title: Iran's soccer team cannot participate in the FIFA World Cup, Iranian minister says
item.description: Iran is set to play three games in the U.S. this June. But amid the U.S.-Israel military campaign that has killed Iran's supreme leader, Iran's sports minister said the team would pull out.
item.content:encoded:
Iran is set to play three games in the U.S. this June. But amid the U.S.-Israel military campaign that has killed Iran's supreme leader, Iran's sports minister said the team would pull out.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Pentagon probe points to U.S. missile hitting Iranian school
item.description: A military assessment suggests a U.S. Tomahawk cruise missile was responsible for at least 165 deaths at an Iranian girls' school, according to a U.S. official who was not authorized to speak publicly.
item.content:encoded:
A military assessment suggests a U.S. Tomahawk cruise missile was responsible for at least 165 deaths at an Iranian girls' school, according to a U.S. official who was not authorized to speak publicly.

(Image credit: Ali Najafii)
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: No Nobles Day: Britain's Parliament boots its last hereditary Lords after 700 years
item.description: Government minister Nick Thomas-Symonds said the change put an end to "an archaic and undemocratic principle." The removed aristocrats are 92 of the House of Lords' 800 members.
item.content:encoded:
Government minister Nick Thomas-Symonds said the change put an end to "an archaic and undemocratic principle." The removed aristocrats are 92 of the House of Lords' 800 members.

(Image credit: Kirsty Wigglesworth)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Greetings from a Shanghai temple where you can ward off bad luck in the Year of the Horse
item.description: According to Chinese mythology, those born in the Year of the Horse will clash with Tai Sui, a heavenly general. Luckily, there are ways to appease Tai Sui, including amulets at Shanghai's Jade Buddha Temple.
item.content:encoded: According to Chinese mythology, those born in the Year of the Horse will clash with Tai Sui, a heavenly general. Luckily, there are ways to appease Tai Sui, including amulets at Shanghai's Jade Buddha Temple.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: Americans skeptical of the Iran war, poll says. And, DOJ gives guns back to felons
item.description: A majority of Americans oppose the U.S.' involvement in the war with Iran, according to a new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll. And, the Department of Justice is quietly restoring gun rights to felons.
item.content:encoded:
A majority of Americans oppose the U.S.' involvement in the war with Iran, according to a new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll. And, the Department of Justice is quietly restoring gun rights to felons.

(Image credit: Christopher Furlong)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Iran war: Cargo ships are hit and U.S. investigates how it could have struck a school
item.description: The Iran war ground through 12th day as uncertainty grew over when it might end, amid continued attacks, a mounting human toll and concerns over the economic and energy impacts.
item.content:encoded:
The Iran war ground through 12th day as uncertainty grew over when it might end, amid continued attacks, a mounting human toll and concerns over the economic and energy impacts.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Four years of war have transformed Russia, but few will speak in public against it
item.description: Russia's full-scale war in Ukraine recently crossed the four-year mark. Throughout that time, one of the biggest questions has been: Is this what Russians want?
item.content:encoded: Russia's full-scale war in Ukraine recently crossed the four-year mark. Throughout that time, one of the biggest questions has been: Is this what Russians want?
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Iran war latest: Hegseth says today marks heaviest bombing yet
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says Tuesday marks the most intense U.S. bombing yet in the Iran war. The 11 days of fighting have spooked oil markets, and the Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed.
item.content:encoded: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says Tuesday marks the most intense U.S. bombing yet in the Iran war. The 11 days of fighting have spooked oil markets, and the Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre
